title: Cost of Capital: A company has a beta of 1.3, a market return...
summary: Calculate the expected return using CAPM with beta, risk-free rate, and market return.
prompt: A company has a beta of 1.3, a market return of 10%, and a risk-free rate of 4%. What is the expected return according to the CAPM?
solution: Expected Return = Risk-Free Rate + Beta × (Market Return - Risk-Free Rate) = 4% + 1.3 × (10% - 4%) = 4% + 1.3 × 6% = 4% + 7.8% = 11.8%. This expected return reflects the risk-adjusted return on equity for the investment.
